2604.10401.title: NameBERT: Scaling Name-Based Nationality Classification with LLM-Augmented Open Academic Data
2604.10401.paper_summary: Inferring nationality from personal names is a critical capability for equity and bias monitoring, personalization, and a valuable tool in biomedical and sociological research. However, existing name-based nationality classifiers are typically trained on relatively small or source-specific labeled datasets, which can introduce coverage gaps and limit performance for underrepresented countries. While large language models (LLMs) demonstrate strong zero-shot performance for name-based nationality prediction, their computational cost and latency make them impractical for real-time, large-scale deployment. In this work, we created a large-scale name-nationality dataset from the Open Academic Graph (OAG) and introduce a framework that leverages LLMs as dataset enrichers rather than inference engines. We augment low-resource countries with LLM-generated names and evaluate on real and synthetic-tail test sets. We find that augmentation produces large gains when evaluation includes synthetic tail names and still offers a modest lift on tail-country metrics otherwise. Overall, NameBERT models achieve significantly higher accuracy than state-of-the-art baselines across both in- and out-of-domain tasks, while remaining efficient for large-scale inference compared to LLMs.
2604.10401.paper_summary_zh: 推斷個人姓名的國籍是進行公平性和偏見監測、個性化以及在生物醫學和社會學研究中一項重要能力。然而，現有的基於姓名的國籍分類器通常是在相對較小或特定來源的標記數據集上訓練的，這可能會導致覆蓋範圍的缺口，並限制對於代表性不足國家的性能。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在基於姓名的國籍預測中顯示出強大的零樣本性能，但其計算成本和延遲使其在實時、大規模部署中不切實際。在這項工作中，我們從開放學術圖譜（OAG）創建了一個大規模的姓名-國籍數據集，並引入了一個利用LLMs作為數據集增強器而非推斷引擎的框架。我們用LLM生成的姓名來增強低資源國家，並在真實和合成尾部測試集上進行評估。我們發現，當評估包括合成尾部姓名時，增強產生了巨大的收益，並且在其他情況下仍然對尾部國家的指標提供了適度的提升。總體而言，NameBERT模型在域內和域外任務中均達到了顯著高於最先進基準的準確性，同時與LLMs相比，在大規模推斷中仍然保持高效。
2604.10401.author: Cong Ming et.al.
2604.10401.authors: Cong Ming, Ruixin Shi, Yifan Hu
